data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Attract $13.17 Million in Net Inflows
data.description: Solana (SOL) spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have garnered significant investor interest, with a net inflow of $13.17 million recorded from February 9 to 13. According to NS3.AI, Bitwise's BSOL led the inflows, contributing $12.72 million, while Fidelity's FSOL added $0.73 million. Despite a minor net outflow from VanEck's VSOL, the total assets of SOL ETFs have increased to $721 million, accounting for 1.5% of Solana's market capitalization.

data.title: Tom Lee Predicts Recovery in Crypto Market by 2026
data.description:
On February 15, Tom Lee shared insights during an interview with Rug Radio founder Farokh Sarmad. According to BlockBeats, Lee described the current crypto market as experiencing a 'mini winter,' suggesting it is not a prolonged bear market but will require time to stabilize. He advised investors to start buying in batches at low points rather than waiting for a perfect bottom, noting that the market's challenges could persist for several months to half a year, with a significant turnaround expected by 2026.

Tom Lee expressed no regrets about purchasing ETH early, stating that ETH is near its bottom, potentially dipping briefly below $1800 or $1890. He highlighted that historically, there have been eight instances of V-shaped recoveries. Lee views ETH as a long-term investment, underpinning stablecoins, AI, and the creator economy over the next 15 years.

Lee also reiterated his prediction that Bitcoin could rise to $200,000-$250,000 by 2026, with Ethereum reaching $12,000-$22,000 based on historical ratios.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Open to UN-Led Temporary Management in Ukraine
data.description: Russia's Deputy Foreign Minister, Galuzin, has expressed willingness to discuss the possibility of introducing a UN-led temporary external management mechanism in Ukraine with the United States, Europe, and other countries. According to Jin10, Galuzin stated that this approach could enable Ukraine to hold democratic elections and establish a government capable of signing peace treaties and other legitimate international cooperation agreements. He noted that similar precedents exist in UN peacekeeping operations. In March 2025, Russian President Vladimir Putin mentioned this as a feasible plan. However, Galuzin also indicated that the possibility of such an approach has not been publicly discussed recently.

data.title: Coinbase Pushes for Direct Fed Access as Reserve Account Debate Intensifies
data.description:

The exchange positions the move as a structural modernization of U. S

data.title: UK Economy Faces Challenges Amid High Inflation, Says BOE's Mann
data.description: The UK economy is experiencing sluggish growth, according to Bank of England rate-setter Catherine Mann. Bloomberg posted on X that Mann described the economic situation as 'tepid,' with consumers being 'scarred' by high inflation, leading to reduced spending. In an interview with the Telegraph, she highlighted that 'key elements' necessary for growth are currently absent.

data.title: Nic Carter warns: If existing Bitcoin developers do not address the quantum risk, institutions may step in and take over Bitcoin development.
data.description: Venture capitalist Nic Carter stated on Thursday's Bits and Bips podcast that if Bitcoin developers fail to address the risks posed by quantum computing quickly, large institutions may lose patience and replace the Bitcoin protocol development team, leading to a "corporate takeover" of Bitcoin. He pointed out that institutions like BlackRock, which manage tens of billions of dollars in Bitcoin assets, will have to take action if the problems remain unresolved for an extended period. According to data, BlackRock currently holds approximately 761,801 BTC (worth about $50.15 billion), representing 3.62% of the total supply. Carter believes that developers are currently being overly passive. However, Ram Ahluwahlia, founder of Lumida Wealth Management, expressed a different view, arguing that major institutions are "passive" investors rather than activists regarding the Bitcoin network. Furthermore, Michael Saylor and others believe the quantum threat has been exaggerated. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs See Mixed Inflows and Outflows in February
data.description: XRP spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced varied financial movements between February 9 and 13. According to NS3.AI, Franklin's XRPZ ETF led the inflows with $5.42 million, contributing to a total net inflow of $7.65 million for the period. Meanwhile, Canary's XRPC ETF also reported significant inflows. In contrast, Grayscale's GXRP ETF faced outflows amounting to nearly $5.9 million. Currently, XRP spot ETFs collectively manage $1.01 billion in assets, which accounts for 1.18% of XRP's total market capitalization.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw net outflows of $360 million this week, marking the fourth consecutive week of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $360 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT experienced the largest net outflow this week, with a weekly net outflow of $235 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow is currently $61.61 billion. This is followed by the Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a weekly net outflow of $125 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow is currently $10.98 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust BTC, with a weekly net inflow of $110 million. BTC's historical total net inflow is currently $2.05 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $87.04 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.33%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $54.33 billion.

data.title: The SOL spot ETF saw a net inflow of $13.1706 million this week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the SOL spot ETF saw a net inflow of $13.1706 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Bitwise ETF BSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $12.7196 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $683 million. This was followed by the Fidelity ETF FSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $732,000, bringing its historical total net inflow to $159 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was the VanEck ETF VSOL, with a weekly net outflow of $554,100, bringing its historical total net inflow to $20.21 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the SOL spot ETF is $721 million, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total SOL market capitalization) is 1.50%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $875 million.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw net outflows of $161 million this week, marking the fourth consecutive week of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $161 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a weekly net outflow of $113 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow is currently $11.98 billion. This was followed by Fidelity ETF FETH, with a weekly net outflow of $40.75 million. FETH's historical total net inflow is currently $2.47 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETH, with a weekly net inflow of $49.9 million. ETH's historical total net inflow is currently $1.71 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $11.72 billion, the ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.75%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.65 billion.

data.title: XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $7.6508 million this week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $7.65 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Franklin ETF XRPZ, with a weekly net inflow of $5.4175 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $328 million. This was followed by the Canary ETF XRPC, with a weekly net inflow of $4.2091 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $413 million. The XRP spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was the Grayscale XRP Trust GXRP, with a weekly net outflow of $5.897 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $131 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF is US$1.01 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of XRP's total market capitalization) is 1.18%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$1.23 billion.
